article_title: China's Yimin Mine Deploys 100 Autonomous Electric Haul Trucks, Setting Three World Records and Eliminating 1,200 Driving Jobs

content_preview.title: China's Yimin Mine Deploys 100 Autonomous Electric Haul Trucks, Setting Three World Records and Eliminating 1,200 Driving Jobs
content_preview.summary: A fleet of 100 Huaneng Ruichi battery-electric haul trucks at an Inner Mongolia coal mine has operated autonomously for ten months, setting records for payload, speed, and cold-weather endurance while cutting 48,000 tonnes of CO2 annually and replacing 300 diesel trucks.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

An open-pit coal mine in Inner Mongolia has become the world's largest deployment of autonomous, battery-electric haul trucks, according to an [Electrek report](https://electrek.co/2026/03/16/this-mine-put-100-autonomous-electric-haul-trucks-to-work-heres-how-it-went/) published March 16. One hundred Huaneng Ruichi trucks, each carrying a 568-kilowatt-hour lithium iron phosphate battery and hauling up to 90 metric tons per load, have been running without human drivers at the Yimin m...

editor_notes:
Well-reported News article on a significant autonomous mining deployment. Summary accurately reflects content. All core claims verified. Minor note: Caterpillar fleet numbers (700 trucks, triple to 2000+ by 2030) are stated as contextual facts not directly attributed to the press release source, but these are widely reported Caterpillar figures. Word count (720) appropriate for News.

source_verification:
- electrek.co: CONFIRMED. 100 Huaneng Ruichi trucks at Yimin mine, 568 kWh LiFePO4 batteries, 90-ton payload, -40C operation, replaced 300 diesel trucks and 1200 drivers, 5-min battery swap at 98% success rate, 120% operational output, Li Shuxue three world records claim.
- interestingengineering.com: CONFIRMED. Huawei 5G-Advanced network, 500 Mbps uplink, 20ms latency, fleet expansion to 300 trucks, 120% efficiency vs manned trucks, -40C operation.
- prnewswire.com: CONFIRMED. Caterpillar-NVIDIA partnership at CES 2026 for physical AI and digital twins in mining/construction equipment. Jetson Thor platform, Cat AI Assistant. Note: specific fleet size numbers not in this press release.
- globenewswire.com: CONFIRMED. Mining automation market $4.56B in 2026, $6.36B by 2030, 8.7% CAGR, key players including Hexagon, Komatsu, Rio Tinto, Caterpillar.
